skill.name: flow-attention-refresh
skill.description: A workflow protocol that keeps AI agents aligned by rereading goal, task, and error documents at key decision points.
skill.summary: Refresh AI attention during long development workflows.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell command execution, but the file contains documentation code fences, YAML-style examples, and diagrams rather than executable code. The weak cryptography and high-entropy alerts also appear to be false positives caused by multilingual front matter and dense non-ASCII documentation. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or executable payload was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fences and workflow examples. They describe reading planning files and focusing on acceptance criteria, but they do not execute commands or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plainly Markdown documentation, including fenced diagrams and YAML-style examples. I found no shell invocation syntax, executable script, or dynamic command construction in these ranges.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static alert points to the skill description in front matter. That line contains natural language about preventing goal drift, not a cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a quoted description field and does not contain crypto APIs or algorithm names. The alert has no supporting semantic evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy From Multilingual Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high-entropy alert appears related to Chinese text and structured Markdown, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content. The file is readable prose throughout.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows legible front matter, headings, tables, and workflow instructions. I found no encoded payload, packed binary data, or obfuscated script.

content.user_title: Refresh AI Focus During Long Workflows
content.value_statement: Long AI development sessions can drift away from the original goal after many tool calls. This skill defines checkpoints that make Claude, Codex, or Claude Code reread goal, task, and error documents before acting.
actual_capabilities: Defines attention refresh checkpoints for flow-style commands.
Instructs the assistant to reread BRAINSTORM.md before major decisions.
Uses TASKS.md to refocus on the current task and acceptance criteria.
Uses ERROR_LOG.md during recovery to avoid repeated mistakes.
Maps refresh protocols to flow-dev, flow-ralph, and related workflows.
Provides stop conditions for missing goal, task, or error context.
limitations: It is a documentation protocol, not an executable automation tool.
It depends on project files such as BRAINSTORM.md, TASKS.md, and ERROR_LOG.md.
It does not create or validate those project files by itself.
It is designed for flow-style workflows and may need adaptation elsewhere.

use_cases.title: Keep Long Coding Tasks Aligned
use_cases.description: Use the protocol before each task so the assistant checks the current goal and definition of done.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using AI coding agents

use_cases.title: Recover From Repeated Build Failures
use_cases.description: Use the error recovery refresh to compare the current failure with recent error records before changing code.
use_cases.target_user: Engineers debugging with Claude Code or Codex

use_cases.title: Coordinate Flow Command Suites
use_cases.description: Use the integration map to connect goal refresh behavior across flow-dev, flow-ralph, and related commands.

prompt_templates.title: Begin a Focused Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Before starting this task, apply the attention refresh protocol. Read the current TASKS.md task and summarize the acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Check Goal Alignment
prompt_templates.scenario: Before a design or implementation decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the major decision refresh. Read the relevant BRAINSTORM.md success criteria, then explain how the next action supports them.

prompt_templates.title: Recover After an Error
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the error recovery refresh. Review the current error, recent ERROR_LOG.md entries, and the active TASKS.md item before proposing a fix.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Iteration Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full attention refresh for this iteration. Identify the next unfinished task, recent repeated errors, target acceptance criteria, and the safest next action.

output_examples.input: Start the next task in TASKS.md.
output_examples.output: The assistant first reviews the active task and definition of done, then states the exact implementation target before editing files.
The assistant records the current failure, checks recent error history, compares it with the current task, and proposes a focused repair plan.
The assistant rereads the project success criteria, names the decision constraints, and explains which option best preserves the original goal.

best_practices: Keep BRAINSTORM.md, TASKS.md, and ERROR_LOG.md concise and current.
Run the refresh before decisions, task starts, and error recovery.
Ask the assistant to restate the active goal before it edits files.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on memory after many tool calls.
Do not skip the refresh because a task appears simple.
Do not fix repeated errors without checking the error log first.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It defines checkpoints that make an AI assistant reread goal, task, and error documents during long workflows.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file is documentation and does not contain executable command logic.

faq.question: Which files does it expect?
faq.answer: It refers to BRAINSTORM.md, TASKS.md, quickstart.md, and ERROR_LOG.md as workflow context files.

faq.question: Can I use it outside flow commands?
faq.answer: Yes, but you may need to adapt the checkpoints to your own task and planning files.

faq.question: How does it help with debugging?
faq.answer: It asks the assistant to review current and recent errors before proposing changes.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found documentation-only workflow guidance and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
